rationale: OpenAPI output must include all required responses and schemas.
OpenAPI schemas must preserve contract field types and requiredness.
Auth requirement/security scheme is mandatory for public endpoints.
operationId must be unique, snake_case, and verb-first.
Breaking API changes must be flagged as compatibility events.
Request/response examples are required.
Docs must follow provider contract, not implementation drift.
Undefined OpenAPI refs are invalid.
Internal-only endpoints should not be published as public API.
No API contract means no invented OpenAPI requirement.
CHANGELOG must follow Keep a Changelog categories.
User guide must include prerequisites and quickstart path.
User-facing docs must not expose internal ADR tradeoffs.
Examples must match approved interface names and paths.
Breaking config/env changes require migration guide.
Release notes must be one nontechnical paragraph plus full summary.
Security findings that affect behavior must be documented.
Changelog entry belongs at top of Unreleased.
Internal refactor should not be user-facing release note bait.
Changelog should not force absent categories.
Docs must never include secrets or PII.
Implementation/spec divergence must route to Spec, not docs implementation.
Coordinator report must list files created/modified.
Docs agent must not write specs or ADRs.
Missing business context for release notes requires human escalation.
Breaking API change not flagged in spec must be raised before documenting.
Docs must not leak prior-feature context.
Docs work must not modify implementation/spec files.
Internal ADR tradeoff omission from user docs is correct.
No user-facing security behavior change should not create scary release notes.
